theme: LIGHT with Neo-Brutalist elements (Dark mode toggle support)

visual_language.surfaces: Use flat solid colors for cards, combined with hard borders and neo-brutalist shadows. No glassmorphism. It should feel like a physical, colorful planner.

components.buttons: Always use neo-brutalist solid background (primary or secondary), hard border, and block shadow. On active/press, translate by 4px so it looks like it's pushed down, and remove the shadow.
components.cards: Solid background, hard border. Header sizes should be H3 or H4. Differentiate subjects using pastel background colors for subject tags/pills.
components.inputs: Shadcn input with border-2 border-black, ring-offset-0. Focus should increase border thickness or change background color to very light yellow.
components.tabs: Override Shadcn tabs to look like physical folder tabs with borders.
components.charts: Recharts. Use hard colors and thick stroke lines (strokeWidth={3}). Hide grid lines or make them very faint dashed lines. Tooltips should have the neo-brutalist style.
components.badges: rounded-full border border-black px-3 py-1 text-xs font-bold uppercase tracking-wider shadow-[2px_2px_0_0_#111111] dark:border-white dark:shadow-[2px_2px_0_0_#FDFDF9]

icons.style: Duotone or Bold weight. We need thick, expressive icons to match the Neo-brutalist vibe.

onboarding_hero.description: Aesthetic study desk with a laptop, sets the mood for focused studying on the onboarding or login screen.

motivation_goal.description: Black and white photo of a doctor's coat and stethoscope. Use in the dashboard or an empty state as a visual reminder of the final goal (Medicina).

exhausted_student.description: A student overwhelmed. Could be used creatively in a 'Don't give up / Take a break' section or error/404 page.

features.dashboard: Bento-style layout. Prominent 'Estudar Hoje' quick-start button. Progress rings/bars for weekly goals.
features.estudar_hoje: Task list with checkboxes. When checked, add a strikethrough animation. Color-code tasks by subject (e.g., Biologia = Mint, Matemática = Blue, Redação = Peach).
features.revisoes: Use Shadcn Accordion or Cards to display 2-week spaced repetition intervals. Visual timelines for each 'frente'.
features.cronograma: Timetable view. Use CSS Grid for hours of the day.
features.questoes_simulados: Recharts for progress. 'Acertos' in mint green, 'Erros' in pastel red/peach.
features.redacao: Score breakdown (C1-C5). Use radar chart or simple thick bar charts.

instructions_to_main_agent: Build the UI focusing heavily on the Neo-Brutalist design language: thick borders (border-2 border-black), solid pastel backgrounds, and sharp drop shadows.
Use 'Outfit' for all headings and 'Figtree' for body text. Ensure you install these fonts.
All interactive elements must have the 'hover:-translate-x-[2px] hover:-translate-y-[2px] active:translate-x-[0px] active:translate-y-[0px]' interaction to simulate a physical button press.
Install and use @phosphor-icons/react with 'weight="bold"' or 'weight="duotone"' to match the bold UI.
Implement Recharts for Simulados and Questões evolution. Keep charts minimalist but bold (thick lines, no soft gradients).
When applying dark mode, invert the black borders/text to white, and use the 'dark' palette colors provided.
Every actionable element MUST have a 'data-testid' attribute.
For form fields in Login/Onboarding, use highly contrasting active states (e.g., focus:bg-yellow-50).
Since it's mobile-first, ensure the bento grid degrades gracefully into a single column with ample spacing (gap-6) and large touch targets (min-h-[48px]).
Ensure UI strings and Date formats perfectly match pt-BR (DD/MM/AAAA).
